description:
You are acting as a newly-hired Site Reliability Engineer (SRE).  
    In the directory /home/user/logs you will find one plain-text file named server_uptime.log.  
    Every line in that file represents the result of an individual 5-minute synthetic health-check in the following **exact** format (fields are space-delimited):

        YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ STATUS RESPONSE_TIME

        • YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ – a UTC ISO-8601 timestamp  
        • STATUS                 – the literal token “OK” or “DOWN”  
        • RESPONSE_TIME          – either a positive integer (milliseconds) when STATUS is OK, **or** the single character “-” when STATUS is DOWN  

    Example line (already present in the file):  
        2023-07-10T00:00:00Z OK 110

    Your job is to create a compact daily report using nothing but the standard GNU tools awk and/or sed (you may chain them with pipes, redirects, etc.).  
    The report consists of two output files that must be written beneath /home/user/reports (create the directory if it does not yet exist):

    1. /home/user/reports/daily_availability.txt  
       • This file must contain exactly **five** newline-terminated lines, in the following order and format (values are examples – you must compute them from the log):  
            Date: 2023-07-10  
            Total Checks: 15  
            OK: 11  
            DOWN: 4  
            Availability: 73.33%  
       • “Availability” is defined as (OK ÷ Total Checks) × 100, rounded to two decimal places.  
       • There must be **no** leading or trailing spaces other than the single space after each colon.

    2. /home/user/reports/slow_responses.txt  
       • Copy, verbatim and in original chronological order, every line from /home/user/logs/server_uptime.log whose STATUS is “OK” **and** numeric RESPONSE_TIME is **greater than 500**.  
       • The output file must contain only those lines and nothing else.  

    Verification notes (follow strictly):  
        • Both files must end with a single trailing newline.  
        • File names and directory paths are case-sensitive; place them exactly as stated.  
        • Use only awk/sed (plus standard UNIX utilities like mkdir, echo, tee, etc.); do not use perl, python, or external CSV/JSON processors.  
        • After you finish, print the absolute paths of both generated files to STDOUT so a human reviewer can quickly open them.  

    Your task is complete when the two report files exist with the specified names, locations, and formats.
truth:

    Exact contents (15 lines, each terminated by a single '\n'):

    Expected artifacts the agent must create:

    1. /home/user/reports/daily_availability.txt  

Date: 2023-07-10
Total Checks: 15
OK: 11
DOWN: 4
Availability: 73.33%

    (exactly 5 lines, each ending in '\n')

    2. /home/user/reports/slow_responses.txt

2023-07-10T00:35:00Z OK 900
2023-07-10T00:45:00Z OK 510

    (exactly 3 lines, each ending in '\n')
